## [Outline]{style="display: block; text-align: center;"}
How should RL model and leverage imperfect human feedback to achieve *human-defined* objectives?

<div style="display: flex; gap: 1em; font-size: 0.8em;">
<div style="flex: 1; border-right: 2px solid #282626; padding-right: 1em;">

**Learning Reward Functions...**

<!-- Learning *ill-defined* rewards from humans -->

1. Feedback Types
2. Aquisition Strategies
3. Tractable Learning

</div>
<div style="flex: 1; padding-left: 1em;">

**...from Imperfect Human Feedback**

<!-- Model the imperfections in human feedback -->

1. Inconsistency
2. Suboptimality
3. Heterogeneity

</div>
</div>

Preference-based deep RL $\rightarrow$ Imperfect human feedback $\rightarrow$ Approaches that address these concerns $\rightarrow$ Active sampling $\rightarrow$ Open problem(s) $\rightarrow$ A proposed solution
